target.name: Aventra
target.founder: Michael Weigand (CEO, co-founder + ex-CGO Shift5; 8 yrs US Army Airborne/Ranger Infantry + first cohort Army Cyber; USMA West Point CS), Brian Retherford (CFO/COO, ex-Army Cyber; USMA + MIT Sloan MBA), Jessup Meng (CRO, ex-Army Cyber; Northeastern Intl Affairs + UW Foster MBA)

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Aventra is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by former US Army Cyber and West Point leadership, having raised $3M to date. The valuation analysis incorporates a peer universe of 10 comparable companies: two public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private firms including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, NODA AI, and Tenna Systems.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $3M raised as a proxy for current value and applying a typical 2.5x Seed-to-next-stage multiplier, yields $8M. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector comparables—spanning Seed through Series A stages—produces a median valuation of $150M, with a range of $60M (Tenna Systems, Seed stage) to $500M (Stark Defence, Series A). The peer median reflects the maturity distribution and later-stage concentration within the comparable set.

Triangulation of both methods supports a valuation range of $6M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $79M. This wide range reflects material uncertainty inherent in early-stage valuation: stage progression anchors to actual capital deployed, while peer comps span both Seed and Series A, introducing stage heterogeneity. Key uncertainties include Aventra's product-market validation relative to peers, revenue traction, and customer acquisition trajectory. The peer-median approach is weighted toward Series A valuations, suggesting caution against over-reliance on the $150M central estimate without evidence of near-term stage progression.
